Further analyses reveal scaling trends in relation to environment diversity and self-evolution rounds, offering insights for building general agent intelligence.
2604.18292.COMMENT: Proposes Agent-World, a self-evolving training arena for general agent intelligence that uses multi-environment RL and dynamic task synthesis to drive continuous agent improvement.
2604.18292.title_cn: Agent-World：扩展现实世界环境合成以发展通用Agent智能
2604.18292.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.18292v1宣布类型：新摘要：人们越来越期望大型语言模型充当与外部有状态工具环境交互的通用代理。模型上下文协议（HCP）和更广泛的代理技能提供了统一的接口，用于将代理与可扩展的现实世界服务连接起来，但由于缺乏现实的环境和终身学习的原则性机制，训练稳健的代理仍然受到限制。在本文中，我们介绍了\textBF{Agent-World}，这是一个自我进化的培训平台，用于通过可扩展的环境推进一般代理智能。Agent-World有两个主要组成部分：（1）抽象环境任务发现，从数千个现实世界环境主题中自主探索主题对齐的数据库和可执行工具生态系统，并合成可验证的任务可控难度;和（2）持续的自我进化的代理训练，它将多环境强化学习与自我结合起来不断发展的代理领域，通过动态任务合成自动识别能力差距并推动有针对性的学习，从而实现代理策略和环境的协同进化。在23个具有挑战性的代理基准中，Agent-World-8B和14 B的表现始终优于强大的专有模型和环境扩展基线。进一步的分析揭示了与环境多样性和自我进化回合相关的扩展趋势，为构建一般代理智能提供见解。

2604.16890.title: Step-GRPO: Internalizing Dynamic Early Exit for Efficient Reasoning
2604.16890.abstract: arXiv:2604.16890v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large reasoning models that use long chain-of-thought excel at problem-solving yet waste compute on redundant checks. Curbing this overthinking is hard: training-time length penalties can cripple ability, while inference-time early-exit adds system overhead. To bridge this gap, we propose Step-GRPO, a novel post-training framework that internalizes dynamic early-exit capabilities directly into the model. Step-GRPO shifts the optimization objective from raw tokens to semantic steps by utilizing linguistic markers to structure reasoning. We introduce a Dynamic Truncated Rollout mechanism that exposes the model to concise high-confidence trajectories during exploration, synergized with a Step-Aware Relative Reward that dynamically penalizes redundancy based on group-level baselines. Extensive experiments across three model sizes on diverse benchmarks demonstrate that Step-GRPO achieves a superior accuracy-efficiency trade-off. On Qwen3-8B, our method reduces token consumption by 32.0\% compared to the vanilla model while avoiding the accuracy degradation observed in traditional length-penalty methods.
2604.16890.COMMENT: Proposes Step-GRPO, a post-training RL framework that internalizes dynamic early-exit for efficient reasoning, directly aligning with RL for LLMs and latent reasoning.
2604.16890.title_cn: Step-GRPO：内化动态早期退出以实现高效推理
2604.16890.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.16890v1宣布类型：新摘要：使用长思想链的大型推理模型擅长解决问题，但会将计算浪费在冗余检查上。遏制这种过度思考是很困难的：训练时间长度的限制可能会削弱能力，而推理时间提前退出则会增加系统负担。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了Step-GRPO，这是一种新的训练后框架，它将动态早期退出能力直接内化到模型中。Step-GRPO通过使用语言标记来结构化推理，将优化目标从原始标记转移到语义步骤。我们引入了动态截断推出机制，该机制在探索过程中将模型暴露于简洁的高置信度轨迹，并与基于组级基线动态惩罚冗余的Step-Aware Relative Reward协同作用。在不同的基准上进行的三种模型大小的广泛实验表明，Step-GRPO实现了卓越的精度-效率权衡。在Qwen 3 -8B上，与vanilla模型相比，我们的方法将代币消耗减少了32.0%，同时避免了传统长度惩罚方法中观察到的准确性下降。

2604.17912.title: Learning to Correct: Calibrated Reinforcement Learning for Multi-Attempt Chain-of-Thought
2604.17912.abstract: arXiv:2604.17912v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: State-of-the-art reasoning models utilize long chain-of-thought (CoT) to solve increasingly complex problems using more test-time computation. In this work, we explore a long CoT setting where the model makes up to K successive attempts at solving a problem, in which each attempt is allowed to build on earlier ones after the model receives a hard verifier feedback. This motivates RL methods that can harness per-attempt rewards by carefully weighting individual attempts. We study optimizing the Verification@K reward (the model succeeds by the K-th attempt) and show that naively weighing the attempts by their pass/fail results in biased gradients. We introduce Calibrated Attempt-Level (CAL) GRPO by devising a weighing strategy to obtain unbiased gradients while maintaining small variance. Our theory reveals how incorporating per-attempt rewards influence the training and the eventual Verification@K performance. Experiments, baselines, and ablations on synthetic and real data corroborate our theory and the benefits of CAL-GRPO over vanilla GRPO as well as naive weighting.
2604.17912.COMMENT: Proposes a calibrated RL method (CAL-GRPO) for optimizing multi-attempt chain-of-thought reasoning using per-attempt verifier feedback, directly aligning with RL for LLMs.
2604.17912.title_cn: 学习纠正：多次尝试思想链的校准强化学习
2604.17912.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17912v1宣布类型：新摘要：最先进的推理模型利用长思想链（CoT）使用更多的测试时计算来解决日益复杂的问题。在这项工作中，我们探索了一个长CoT设置，其中模型在解决问题时进行了多达K次连续尝试，其中在模型收到硬验证者反馈后，允许每次尝试在早期尝试的基础上进行构建。这激励了RL方法，这些方法可以通过仔细加权个人尝试来利用每次尝试的奖励。我们研究优化Verification@K奖励（该模型通过第K次尝试成功），并表明天真地通过通过/失败来权衡尝试会导致有偏差的梯度。我们通过设计加权策略引入校准Attempt-Level（CAL）GRPO，以获得无偏梯度，同时保持较小的方差。我们的理论揭示了纳入每次尝试奖励如何影响训练和最终的Verification@K性能。对合成和真实数据的实验、基线和消融证实了我们的理论以及Calib-GRPO相对于普通GRPO以及朴素加权的优势。

2604.17406.title: EvoMaster: A Foundational Agent Framework for Building Evolving Autonomous Scientific Agents at Scale
2604.17406.abstract: arXiv:2604.17406v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: The convergence of large language models and agents is catalyzing a new era of scientific discovery: Agentic Science. While the scientific method is inherently iterative, existing agent frameworks are predominantly static, narrowly scoped, and lack the capacity to learn from trial and error. To bridge this gap, we present EvoMaster, a foundational evolving agent framework engineered specifically for Agentic Science at Scale. Driven by the core principle of continuous self-evolution, EvoMaster empowers agents to iteratively refine hypotheses, self-critique, and progressively accumulate knowledge across experimental cycles, faithfully mirroring human scientific inquiry. Crucially, as a domain-agnostic base harness, EvoMaster is exceptionally easy to scale up -- enabling developers to build and deploy highly capable, self-evolving scientific agents for arbitrary disciplines in approximately 100 lines of code. Built upon EvoMaster, we incubated the SciMaster ecosystem across domains such as machine learning, physics, and general science. Evaluations on four authoritative benchmarks (Humanity's Last Exam, MLE-Bench Lite, BrowseComp, and FrontierScience) demonstrate that EvoMaster achieves state-of-the-art scores of 41.1%, 75.8%, 73.3%, and 53.3%, respectively. It comprehensively outperforms the general-purpose baseline OpenClaw with relative improvements ranging from +159% to +316%, robustly validating its efficacy and generality as the premier foundational framework for the next generation of autonomous scientific discovery. EvoMaster is available at https://github.com/sjtu-sai-agents/EvoMaster.
2604.17406.COMMENT: Introduces EvoMaster, a foundational framework for building self-evolving scientific agents that iteratively refine hypotheses and self-critique, directly matching the self-improving agent criterion.
2604.17406.title_cn: EvoMaster：用于大规模构建不断发展的自主科学代理的基础代理框架
2604.17406.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17406v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型语言模型和代理的融合正在催化科学发现的新时代：统计科学。虽然科学方法本质上是迭代的，但现有的代理框架主要是静态的、范围狭窄的，并且缺乏从试错中学习的能力。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了EvoMaster，这是一个专门为大规模统计科学而设计的基础性进化代理框架。在持续自我进化的核心原则的驱动下，EvoMaster使代理能够迭代完善假设、自我批评并在实验周期中逐步积累知识，忠实地反映人类的科学探究。至关重要的是，作为一种领域不可知的基础工具，EvoMaster非常容易扩展-使开发人员能够在大约100行代码中为任意学科构建和部署高能力、自我进化的科学代理。我们以EvoMaster为基础，孵化了跨越机器学习、物理和普通科学等领域的SciMaster生态系统。对四个权威基准（Humanity ' s Last Exam、MLE-Bench Lite、BrowseComp和FrontierScience）的评估表明，EvoMaster的最先进成绩分别为41.1%、75.8%、73.3%和53.3%。它全面优于通用基线OpenClaw，相对改进范围为+159%至+316%，有力地验证了其作为下一代自主科学发现首要基础框架的有效性和通用性。EvoMaster可在https://github.com/sjtu-sai-agents/EvoMaster上获取。

2604.17073.title: Abstain-R1: Calibrated Abstention and Post-Refusal Clarification via Verifiable RL
2604.17073.abstract: arXiv:2604.17073v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Reinforcement fine-tuning improves the reasoning ability of large language models, but it can also encourage them to answer unanswerable queries by guessing or hallucinating missing information. Existing abstention methods either train models to produce generic refusals or encourage follow-up clarifications without verifying whether those clarifications identify the key missing information. We study queries that are clear in meaning but cannot be reliably resolved from the given information, and argue that a reliable model should not only abstain, but also explain what is missing. We propose a clarification-aware RLVR reward that, while rewarding correct answers on answerable queries, jointly optimizes explicit abstention and semantically aligned post-refusal clarification on unanswerable queries. Using this reward, we train Abstain-R1, a 3B model that improves abstention and clarification on unanswerable queries while preserving strong performance on answerable ones. Experiments on Abstain-Test, Abstain-QA, and SelfAware show that Abstain-R1 substantially improves over its base model and achieves unanswerable-query behavior competitive with larger systems including DeepSeek-R1, suggesting that calibrated abstention and clarification can be learned through verifiable rewards rather than emerging from scale alone.
2604.17073.COMMENT: Proposes a verifiable RL reward (RLVR) to jointly optimize explicit abstention and post-refusal clarification for LLMs, directly matching RL for LLMs criteria.
2604.17073.title_cn: Abstain-R1：通过可验证RL校准的弃权和拒绝后澄清
2604.17073.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17073v1公告类型：新摘要：强化微调提高了大型语言模型的推理能力，但它也会鼓励它们通过猜测或幻觉缺失的信息来回答无法回答的查询。现有的排除方法要么训练模型来产生一般性的拒绝，要么鼓励后续的澄清，而不验证这些澄清是否识别了关键的缺失信息。我们研究含义明确但无法从给定信息可靠地解决的查询，并认为可靠的模型不仅应该弃权，还应该解释缺失的内容。我们提出了一种描述感知的WLVR奖励，在奖励可回答查询的正确答案的同时，联合优化了对不可回答查询的显式描述和语义对齐的拒绝后澄清。使用这个奖励，我们训练了Abstain-R1，这是一个3B模型，可以改善对无法回答的查询的描述和澄清，同时在可回答的查询上保持强劲的性能。Abstain-Test、Abstain-QA和SelfAware的实验表明，Abstain-R1比其基本模型有了大幅改进，并实现了与DeepSeek-R1等大型系统竞争的无答案查询行为，这表明校准的描述和澄清可以通过可验证的奖励来学习，而不是单独从规模中产生。

2604.17935.title: How Much Cache Does Reasoning Need? Depth-Cache Tradeoffs in KV-Compressed Transformers
2604.17935.abstract: arXiv:2604.17935v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: The key-value (KV) cache is the dominant memory bottleneck during Transformer inference, yet little is known theoretically about how aggressively it can be compressed before multi-step reasoning degrades. We study this through $k$-hop pointer chasing on $n$ tokens under a shared KV cache of size $s$, attention dimension $m$, $H$ heads, $p$-bit precision, and a locality-respecting cache controller (satisfied by all standard KV-compression methods). We give three results.   (1) Product depth lower bound (conjectured). We conjecture that any such Transformer ($n \geq 4k$, $s \leq \sqrt{n}/4$) requires depth $L = \Omega(\lceil k/s \rceil \cdot \lceil \log_2 n/(Hmp) \rceil)$, and isolate the sole remaining gap as a probabilistic step on the joint distribution of cache trace and pointer chain. Unconditionally, we prove a matching upper bound $L = O(\min(k, \lceil k/s \rceil \log s) \cdot \log n/(mp))$ via windowed pointer doubling, and a max-bound $L = \Omega(\max(\lceil k/s \rceil, \log n/(Hmp)))$. Closing the conjecture amounts to upgrading max to product.   (2) Bandwidth barrier. The product bound binds only when $Hmp \lesssim \log n$. Any lower bound provable via per-window distinguishability counting -- including reachability, bandwidth, and combinations -- cannot exceed $\lceil k/s \rceil$ once $Hmp \geq \log_2 n$. Breaking this requires lifting unconditional communication-complexity bounds for pointer chasing to Cache-Transformer depth.   (3) Adaptive vs oblivious error scaling. Under random cache over $T = \lceil \log_2 k \rceil$ doubling stages, oblivious caches give $\Pr[\mathcal{E}] \leq (s/(n-T))^T + 2T^3/n$ (exponential in $T$), while adaptive locality-respecting caches achieve $\Pr[\mathcal{E}] = s/n$ exactly, independent of $T$. The $\Omega((n/s)^{T-1})$ separation explains why heavy-hitter eviction empirically dominates random eviction for multi-hop reasoning.
2604.17935.COMMENT: Provides a theoretical analysis of KV-cache compression for multi-step reasoning, directly relevant to latent reasoning efficiency by studying depth-cache tradeoffs in Transformers.
2604.17935.title_cn: 推理需要多少缓存？KV压缩变压器中的深度缓存权衡
2604.17935.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17935v1宣布类型：新摘要：key-Value（KV）缓存是Transformer推理过程中的主要内存瓶颈，但理论上对于在多步推理降级之前可以如何积极地压缩它知之甚少。我们通过在大小为$s $、注意力维度为$m $、$H $头、$p $-位精度和尊重本地化的缓存控制器（所有标准GV压缩方法都满足）下对$n $令牌进行$k $-跳指针追逐来研究这一点。我们给出三个结果。   (1)产品深度下限（假设）。我们推测任何这样的Transformer（$n\geq 4k $，$s\leq\SQRT {n}/4 $）都需要深度$L =\Omega（\lceil k/s\rceil\csot\lceil\log_2 n/（Hmp）\rceil）$，并隔离唯一剩余的间隙作为缓存跟踪和指针链联合分布的概率步骤。无条件地，我们证明了匹配的上限$L = O（\min（k，\lceil k/s\rceil\log s）\cdot\log n/（mp））$通过窗口指针加倍，和最大界$L =\Omega（\max（\lceil k/s\rceil，\log n/（Hmp））$。结束这个猜测相当于将max升级为产品。   (2)带宽障碍。仅当$Hmp\lesssim\log n $时，绑定的产品才会绑定。通过每窗口可达性计数（包括可达性、带宽和组合）可证明的任何下限都不能超过$\lceil k/s\rceil $一次$Hmp\geq\log_2 n $。打破这一点需要取消无条件的通信复杂性界限，以使指针追逐到Cache-Transformer深度。   (3)自适应与无意识误差缩放。在$T =\lceil\log_2 k\rceil $双倍阶段上的随机缓存下，不经意缓存给出$\Pr [\mathCal {E}]\leq（s/（n-T）' T +2T' 3/n $（指数单位为$T $），而自适应本地化缓存则实现$\Pr [\mathCal {E}]= s/n $，与$T $无关。$\Omega（n/s）' s）' s ' s）$分离解释了为什么重型驱逐在多跳推理中占主导地位。

2604.16484.title: DexWorldModel: Causal Latent World Modeling towards Automated Learning of Embodied Tasks
2604.16484.abstract: arXiv:2604.16484v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Deploying generative World-Action Models for manipulation is severely bottlenecked by redundant pixel-level reconstruction, $\mathcal{O}(T)$ memory scaling, and sequential inference latency. We introduce the Causal Latent World Model (CLWM), which employs DINOv3 features as generative targets to disentangle interaction semantics from visual noise, yielding highly robust domain generalization. To overcome memory scaling, CLWM features a Dual-State Test-Time Training (TTT) Memory that guarantees a strict $\mathcal{O}(1)$ footprint for long-horizon tasks. To overcome deployment latency, we propose Speculative Asynchronous Inference (SAI) to mask partial diffusion denoising behind physical execution, cutting blocking latency by about $50\%$. To scale robust policies, we present EmbodiChain, an online framework that establishes the Efficiency Law by injecting an infinite flow of physics-grounded trajectories during training. Extensive experiments validate that CLWM achieves state-of-the-art performance in complex dual-arm simulation and unprecedented zero-shot sim-to-real transfer on physical robots, outperforming baselines explicitly finetuned on real-world data.
2604.16484.COMMENT: Proposes EmbodiChain, an online framework for automated learning of embodied tasks via an infinite flow of physics-grounded trajectories, directly aligning with self-evolving agent criteria.
2604.16484.title_cn: DexWorldModel：面向预定任务自动学习的因果潜在世界建模
2604.16484.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.16484v1宣布类型：新摘要：部署生成式世界动作模型进行操纵会受到冗余像素级重建、$\mathcal{O}（T）$内存缩放和顺序推理延迟的严重干扰。我们引入了因果潜在世界模型（CLWM），该模型采用DINOv 3特征作为生成目标，将交互语义与视觉噪音区分开来，从而产生高度稳健的领域概括。为了克服内存扩展问题，CLWM具有双状态测试时间训练（TTT）内存，可保证长期任务的严格$\mathCal{O}（1）$占用空间。为了克服部署延迟，我们提出推测性同步推理（SAI）来掩盖物理执行背后的部分扩散去噪，从而将阻塞延迟减少约50美元。为了扩展稳健的政策，我们提出了DeliverChain，这是一个在线框架，通过在训练期间注入无限流的基于物理的轨迹来建立效率定律。大量实验证实，CLWM在复杂的双臂模拟和物理机器人上前所未有的零镜头实时传输中实现了最先进的性能，超过了根据现实世界数据明确调整的基线。

2604.16358.title: SaFeR-Steer: Evolving Multi-Turn MLLMs via Synthetic Bootstrapping and Feedback Dynamics
2604.16358.abstract: arXiv:2604.16358v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: MLLMs are increasingly deployed in multi-turn settings, where attackers can escalate unsafe intent through the evolving visual-text history and exploit long-context safety decay. Yet safety alignment is still dominated by single-turn data and fixed-template dialogues, leaving a mismatch between training and deployment.To bridge this gap, we propose SaFeR-Steer, a progressive multi-turn alignment framework that combines staged synthetic bootstrapping with tutor-in-the-loop GRPO to train a single student under adaptive, on-policy attacks. We also introduce TCSR, which uses trajectory minimum/average safety to propagate late-turn failures to earlier turns.I. Dataset. We release STEER, a multi-turn multimodal safety dataset with STEER-SFT (12,934), STEER-RL (2,000), and STEER-Bench (3,227) dialogues spanning 2~10 turns.II. Experiment. Starting from Qwen2.5-VL-3B/7B, SaFeR-Steer substantially improves Safety/Helpfulness on both single-turn (48.30/45.86 -> 81.84/70.77 for 3B; 56.21/60.32 -> 87.89/77.40 for 7B) and multi-turn benchmarks (12.55/27.13 -> 55.58/70.27 for 3B; 24.66/46.48 -> 64.89/72.35 for 7B), shifting failures to later turns and yielding robustness beyond scaling alone.Codes are available at https://github.com/Ed-Bg/SaFeR-Steer
2604.16358.COMMENT: Proposes a multi-turn alignment framework using synthetic bootstrapping and on-policy RL (GRPO), directly relevant to RL for LLMs with a focus on safety.
2604.16358.title_cn: SaFeR-Steer：通过合成引导和反馈动力学开发多圈MLLM
2604.16358.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.16358v1宣布类型：新摘要：MLLM越来越多地部署在多回合环境中，攻击者可以通过不断变化的视觉文本历史记录升级不安全意图，并利用长上下文安全衰退。然而，安全对齐仍然由单回合数据和固定模板对话主导，导致培训和部署之间存在不匹配。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了SaFeR-Steer，这是一个渐进的多回合对齐框架，它将分阶段合成引导与导师在环GRPO相结合，以在自适应的、按政策攻击下训练单个学生。我们还引入了TCSR，它使用轨迹最小/平均安全性将后期转弯故障传播到早期转弯。I.数据集。我们发布了STEER，这是一个多回合多模式安全数据集，包含STEER-SFT（12，934）、STEER-RL（2，000）和STEER-Bench（3，227）对话，跨越2~10回合。II。实验SaFeR-Steer从Qwen 2.5-BL-3B/7 B开始，大幅提高了单圈的安全性/帮助性（3B为48.30/45.86 -> 81.84/70.77; 7 B为56.21/60.32 -> 87.89/77.40）和多圈基准（3B为12.55/27.13 -> 55.58/70.27; 7 B为24.66/46.48 -> 64.89/72.35），将故障转移到后期，并产生超出单独扩展范围的稳健性。代码可在www.example.com上获取https://github.com/Ed-Bg/SaFeR-Steer

2604.18176.title: QuantumQA: Enhancing Scientific Reasoning via Physics-Consistent Dataset and Verification-Aware Reinforcement Learning
2604.18176.abstract: arXiv:2604.18176v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) show strong capabilities in general reasoning but typically lack reliability in scientific domains like quantum mechanics, which demand strict adherence to physical constraints. This limitation arises from the scarcity of verifiable training resources and the inadequacy of coarse feedback signals in standard alignment paradigms. To address the data challenge, we introduce QuantumQA, a large-scale dataset constructed via a task-adaptive strategy and a hybrid verification protocol that combines deterministic solvers with semantic auditing to guarantee scientific rigor. Building on this foundation, we propose the verification-aware reward model (VRM) tailored for Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR), which employs an adaptive reward fusion (ARF) mechanism to dynamically integrate deterministic signals from a scientific execution suite (SES) with multidimensional semantic evaluations for precise supervision. Experimental results demonstrate that our method consistently outperforms baselines and general-purpose preference models. Notably, our optimized 8B model achieves performance competitive with proprietary models, validating that incorporating verifiable, rule-based feedback into the reinforcement learning loop offers a parameter-efficient alternative to pure scaling.
2604.18176.COMMENT: Proposes a verification-aware reward model and RL with verifiable rewards (RLVR) to improve LLM scientific reasoning, directly aligning with RL for LLMs.
2604.18176.title_cn: QuantumQA：通过物理一致的数据集和验证意识强化学习增强科学推理
2604.18176.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.18176v1公告类型：新摘要：大型语言模型（LLM）在一般推理方面表现出强大的能力，但在量子力学等科学领域通常缺乏可靠性，这些领域需要严格遵守物理约束。这种限制源于可验证的训练资源的稀缺以及标准对齐范式中粗略反馈信号的不足。为了应对数据挑战，我们引入了QuantumQA，这是一个通过任务自适应策略和混合验证协议构建的大规模数据集，该协议将确定性求解器与语义审计相结合，以保证科学的严谨性。在此基础上，我们提出了为具有可验证奖励的强化学习（WLVR）量身定制的验证感知奖励模型（VRM），该模型采用自适应奖励融合（ARF）机制来动态集成来自科学执行套件（SES）的确定性信号与多维语义评估，以实现精确监督。实验结果表明，我们的方法始终优于基线和通用偏好模型。值得注意的是，我们优化的8B模型实现了与专有模型具有竞争力的性能，验证了将可验证的、基于规则的反馈整合到强化学习循环中提供了纯扩展的参数高效替代方案。

2604.17328.title: Rethinking the Comparison Unit in Sequence-Level Reinforcement Learning: An Equal-Length Paired Training Framework from Loss Correction to Sample Construction
2604.17328.abstract: arXiv:2604.17328v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: This paper investigates the length problem in sequence-level relative reinforcement learning. We observe that, although existing methods partially alleviate length-related phenomena, a more fundamental issue remains insufficiently characterized: the comparison units used during training lack inherent comparability. Building on this observation, we propose a new perspective: the length problem should not be viewed merely as a loss-scaling or normalization bias, but rather as a \emph{comparison unit construction} problem. We further establish a sample-construction-based training framework that, instead of applying post-hoc corrections to unequal-length responses, proactively constructs equal-length, alignable, and comparable training segments during generation. Within this framework, we propose EqLen, a concrete method applicable to group-relative comparison algorithms such as GRPO, GSPO, and RLOO. Through dual-track synchronous generation, prefix inheritance, and segment masking, EqLen efficiently collects effective equal-length training segments and enables stable
2604.17328.COMMENT: Proposes a new training framework (EqLen) for sequence-level RL in LLMs, directly addressing the length bias problem in reward comparison for methods like GRPO and RLOO.
2604.17328.title_cn: 重新思考序列级强化学习中的比较单元：从损失纠正到样本构建的长度成对训练框架
2604.17328.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17328v1宣布类型：新摘要：本文研究了序列级相对强化学习中的长度问题。我们观察到，尽管现有方法部分缓解了与长度相关的现象，但一个更根本的问题仍然没有充分描述：训练期间使用的比较单位缺乏固有的可比性。在这一观察的基础上，我们提出了一个新的观点：长度问题不应仅仅被视为损失缩放或规范化偏差，而应被视为一个\{比较单元构造}问题。我们进一步建立了一个基于样本构建的训练框架，该框架不是对不等长响应应用事后纠正，而是在生成过程中主动构建等长、可预测且可比的训练片段。在这个框架下，我们提出了EqLen，这是一种适用于GRPO、GSPO和RLOO等群体相对比较算法的具体方法。EqLen通过双轨同步生成、前置继承和分段掩蔽，高效收集有效的等长训练分段，实现稳定

2604.17691.title: SafeAnchor: Preventing Cumulative Safety Erosion in Continual Domain Adaptation of Large Language Models
2604.17691.abstract: arXiv:2604.17691v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Safety alignment in large language models is remarkably shallow: it is concentrated in the first few output tokens and reversible by fine-tuning on as few as 100 adversarial examples. This fragility becomes critical in real-world deployment, where models undergo sequential adaptation across domains such as medicine, law, and code, causing safety guardrails to erode cumulatively. Yet all existing safety-preserving methods target only single-task fine-tuning, leaving the multi-domain sequential setting entirely unaddressed.   We introduce SafeAnchor, a framework that anchors safety in place throughout continual adaptation. SafeAnchor first identifies low-rank safety subspaces in LoRA parameter space via Fisher Information eigendecomposition, then constrains domain-specific gradient updates to the orthogonal complement of these subspaces, and finally monitors for residual safety drift with threshold-triggered corrective replay. Evaluated on Llama-2-7B-Chat and Mistral-7B-Instruct across a three-domain pipeline and eight benchmarks, SafeAnchor retains 93.2% of original safety alignment, outperforming all baselines by 18-42 points, while matching unconstrained fine-tuning to within 1.5 points on domain tasks.
2604.17691.COMMENT: Proposes a novel method (SafeAnchor) to prevent safety erosion in LLMs during continual domain adaptation, directly addressing a core RL/alignment problem via low-rank safety subspace anchoring and corrective replay.
2604.17691.title_cn: SafeAnchor：防止大型语言模型连续领域适应中的累积安全侵蚀
2604.17691.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17691v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型语言模型中的安全一致非常浅：它集中在前几个输出标记中，并通过对少至100个对抗性示例进行微调而可逆。这种脆弱性在现实世界的部署中变得至关重要，模型会在医学、法律和代码等领域进行顺序调整，导致安全护栏被累积侵蚀。然而，所有现有的安全保护方法都仅针对单任务微调，而完全没有解决多域顺序设置问题。   我们引入SafeAnchor，这是一个在持续调整过程中将安全锚定在适当位置的框架。SafeAnchor首先通过Fisher信息特征分解识别LoRA参数空间中的低等级安全子空间，然后将特定于域的梯度更新约束为这些子空间的垂直补，最后通过阈值触发的纠正重播来监控剩余安全漂移。SafeAnchor在Llama-2- 7 B-Chat和Mistral-7 B-Direct上进行了三个域管道和八个基准的评估，保留了93.2%的原始安全对齐，比所有基线高出18-42个百分点，同时将不受限制的微调匹配到领域任务的1.5个百分点以内。

2604.17774.title: Prompt Optimization Enables Stable Algorithmic Collusion in LLM Agents
2604.17774.abstract: arXiv:2604.17774v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: LLM agents in markets present algorithmic collusion risks. While prior work shows LLM agents reach supracompetitive prices through tacit coordination, existing research focuses on hand-crafted prompts. The emerging paradigm of prompt optimization necessitates new methodologies for understanding autonomous agent behavior. We investigate whether prompt optimization leads to emergent collusive behaviors in market simulations. We propose a meta-learning loop where LLM agents participate in duopoly markets and an LLM meta-optimizer iteratively refines shared strategic guidance. Our experiments reveal that meta-prompt optimization enables agents to discover stable tacit collusion strategies with substantially improved coordination quality compared to baseline agents. These behaviors generalize to held-out test markets, indicating discovery of general coordination principles. Analysis of evolved prompts reveals systematic coordination mechanisms through stable shared strategies. Our findings call for further investigation into AI safety implications in autonomous multi-agent systems.
2604.17774.COMMENT: Proposes a meta-learning loop where LLM agents evolve stable collusive strategies through prompt optimization, directly aligning with self-improving agent frameworks.
2604.17774.title_cn: 即时优化实现LLM代理中稳定的种族共谋
2604.17774.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17774v1宣布类型：新摘要：市场中的LLM代理存在算法共谋风险。虽然之前的工作表明LLM代理通过隐性协调达到超竞争性的价格，但现有的研究重点是手工制作的提示。新兴的即时优化范式需要新的方法来理解自主代理行为。我们调查即时优化是否会导致市场模拟中出现的共谋行为。我们提出了一个元学习循环，其中LLM代理参与双寡头市场，LLM元优化器迭代完善共享的战略指导。我们的实验表明，元提示优化使代理能够发现稳定的隐性共谋策略，与基线代理相比，协调质量显着提高。这些行为普遍适用于持有的测试市场，表明普遍协调原则的发现。对进化提示的分析揭示了通过稳定的共享策略的系统协调机制。我们的研究结果呼吁进一步调查人工智能对自主多智能体系统的安全影响。

2604.17299.title: Cat-DPO: Category-Adaptive Safety Alignment
2604.17299.abstract: arXiv:2604.17299v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Aligning large language models with human preferences must balance two competing goals: responding helpfully to legitimate requests and reliably refusing harmful ones. Most preference-based safety alignment methods collapse safety into a single scalar that is applied uniformly to every preference pair. The result is a model that looks safe on average but stays relatively unsafe on a minority of harm categories. We cast safety alignment as a per-category constrained optimization problem and derive Cat-DPO, a direct-preference-optimization algorithm with a separate adaptive safety margin for each harm category. The margin tightens when the model still produces unsafe responses on a category and relaxes once the model catches up, so the training signal tracks each category's current difficulty rather than averaging under one global rate. Across two LLM backbones and six preference-learning baselines, Cat-DPO iimproves aggregate helpfulness and harmlessness and compresses per-category safety variance and the best-to-worst gap, offering a drop-in per-category refinement of direct preference safety alignment.
2604.17299.COMMENT: Proposes Cat-DPO, a direct-preference-optimization algorithm with per-category adaptive safety margins, directly improving LLM safety alignment via a novel reward design.
2604.17299.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17299v1宣布类型：新摘要：将大型语言模型与人类偏好保持一致必须平衡两个相互竞争的目标：对合法请求做出有益的响应和可靠地拒绝有害请求。大多数基于偏好的安全对齐方法将安全性分解为一个单一的纯量，该纯量统一应用于每个偏好对。结果是，模型平均看起来安全，但在少数伤害类别上保持相对不安全。我们将安全对齐视为按类别约束优化问题，并推导出Cat-DPO，这是一种直接偏好优化算法，为每个伤害类别都有单独的自适应安全裕度。当模型仍然对某个类别产生不安全的响应时，差距就会收紧，而一旦模型赶上，差距就会放松，因此训练信号会跟踪每个类别的当前难度，而不是在一个全球率下求平均值。在两个LLM主干和六个偏好学习基线中，Cat-DPO提高了总体有益性和无害性，并压缩了每类别的安全差异和最好到最坏的差距，提供了每类别的直接偏好安全性调整的直接优化。

2604.16694.title: RankGuide: Tensor-Rank-Guided Routing and Steering for Efficient Reasoning
2604.16694.abstract: arXiv:2604.16694v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large reasoning models (LRMs) enhance problem-solving capabilities by generating explicit multi-step chains of thought (CoT) reasoning; however, they incur substantial inference latency and computational overhead. To mitigate this issue, recent works have explored model collaboration paradigms, where small reasoning models (SRMs) generate intermediate reasoning steps to achieve a better accuracy--latency trade-off. Despite recent progress, effectively and efficiently detecting and mitigating SRM failures in collaborative systems remains a key challenge. To address this issue, we analyze SRM inference in both the generated text and hidden-state spaces, and identify three types of failure modes: \textit{overconfidence}, \textit{uncertainty}, and \textit{heavy revalidation}. Building on these insights, we propose \textbf{RankGuide}, a framework that improves the efficiency and effectiveness of SRM--LRM collaboration through tensor-rank-guided routing and steering. Specifically, RankGuide leverages a routing signal that incorporates tensor-rank signals derived from consecutive hidden states to detect when SRMs are likely to fail and selectively invoke LRMs. In addition, we introduce a tensor-rank-filtered steering vector extraction method to modulate the reasoning trajectory of SRMs, thereby improving their generation quality. By improving both routing and steering through tensor-rank signals, RankGuide enables SRM--LRM collaborative systems to achieve more efficient reasoning with fewer steps and improved accuracy. Experiments on multiple reasoning benchmarks demonstrate the efficacy of RankGuide in reducing latency by up to $1.75\times$ compared to LRM, while maintaining competitive accuracy relative to prior methods.
2604.16694.COMMENT: Proposes a tensor-rank-guided routing and steering framework to improve efficiency and accuracy in collaborative reasoning between small and large models.
2604.16694.title_cn: RankGuide：张量排名引导的路由和引导，以实现高效推理
2604.16694.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.16694v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型推理模型（LRM）通过生成显式的多步思维链（CoT）推理来增强解决问题的能力;然而，它们会带来大量的推理延迟和计算费用。为了缓解这个问题，最近的工作探索了模型协作范式，其中小型推理模型（SRC）生成中间推理步骤以实现更好的准确性--延迟权衡。尽管最近取得了进展，但有效且高效地检测和缓解协作系统中的CRM故障仍然是一个关键挑战。为了解决这个问题，我们在生成的文本和隐藏状态空间中分析了SOM推断，并识别了三种类型的故障模式：\texttit {overconcidence}、\texttit {incidence}和\texttit {heavy revalidation}。在这些见解的基础上，我们提出了\textBF{RankGuide}，这是一个框架，通过张量排名引导的路由和转向来提高SRM-LRM协作的效率和有效性。具体来说，RankGuide利用包含从连续隐藏状态推导出的张量等级信号的路由信号来检测SRM何时可能失败并选择性地调用LRM。此外，我们还引入了一种张量排序过滤的引导量提取方法来调节SR RM的推理轨迹，从而提高其生成质量。通过通过张量排序信号改进路由和引导，RankGuide使SRM-LRM协作系统能够以更少的步骤和更高的准确性实现更高效的推理。针对多个推理基准的实验证明，与LRM相比，RankGuide在将延迟减少高达1.75美元\x $方面的功效，同时保持相对于先前方法的有竞争力的准确性。

2604.17937.title: ContraPrompt: Contrastive Prompt Optimization via Dyadic Reasoning Trace Analysis
2604.17937.abstract: arXiv:2604.17937v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Prompt optimization methods either analyze individual failures in isolation or compare prompt variants across examples, operating on single execution traces with no access to the reasoning process distinguishing success from failure on the same input. We introduce ContraPrompt, built on the observation that when a model fails but succeeds on a retry with feedback, the difference between its two chain-of-thought traces constitutes an optimization signal not captured by prior methods. Unlike prior contrastive methods, we compare complete intermediate reasoning processes: the two traces share model, input, and base prompt, so remaining differences reflect reasoning strategy and appended error feedback -- we call this dyadic reasoning trace analysis. The multi-attempt solving phase is an instrumented agentic retry loop that generates contrastive data automatically without human annotation. Extracted rules are organized into an input-aware decision tree routing instructions by observable input characteristics. On four reasoning and compliance benchmarks, ContraPrompt outperforms GEPA (Agrawal et al., 2026) on all four, with absolute gains of +8.29 pp on HotPotQA (+20.8% rel.), +2.21 pp on GDPR-Bench (+18.2% rel.), +7.14 pp on GPQA Diamond (+10.6% rel.), and +0.74 pp on BBH (+0.85% rel.). Ablations confirm dyadic trace contrastivity is the critical component, with a -16% relative average drop upon its removal. On 53 EvalSet black-box optimization problems, ContraPrompt beats GEPA on 11, ties on 41, and loses on 1 at equal budget. On FiNER-139 financial named entity recognition (Loukas et al., 2022), ContraPrompt achieves +7.77 pp over the unoptimized baseline (+11.6% rel.) and +1.94 pp over GEPA (+2.66% rel.), with branch conditions aligning with standard US GAAP financial-instrument categories.
2604.17937.COMMENT: Introduces a self-improving agent framework (ContraPrompt) that uses dyadic reasoning trace analysis from retry loops to optimize prompts, aligning with self-evolving agents.
2604.17937.title_cn: contrast Promote：通过二元推理痕迹分析进行对比提示优化
2604.17937.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17937v1宣布类型：新摘要：提示优化方法要么孤立地分析单个失败，要么比较跨示例的提示变体，对单个执行轨迹进行操作，而无法访问区分同一输入上的成功与失败的推理过程。我们引入了Contrapply，它建立在这样的观察基础上，即当模型失败但通过反馈成功地尝试时，其两个思想链轨迹之间的差异构成了先前方法无法捕捉到的优化信号。与之前的对比方法不同，我们比较了完整的中间推理过程：两个轨迹共享模型、输入和基本提示，因此剩余的差异反映了推理策略和附加的错误反馈--我们称之为二元推理轨迹分析。多次尝试求解阶段是一个工具化的代理再试循环，可以自动生成对比数据，无需人工注释。提取的规则通过可观察的输入特征组织成输入感知决策树路由指令。在四个推理和合规性基准方面，Contrapt优于GEPA（Agrawal等人，2026年），HotPotQA上的绝对收益为+8.29 pp（+20.8%），GDPR-Bench上+2.21 pp（+18.2%），GPQA Diamond上+7.14页（+10.6%相对值），BBH上+0.74 pp（+0.85%相对值）。烧蚀证实二元痕迹对比度是关键组成部分，去除后相对平均下降-16%。在53个EvalSet黑匣子优化问题上，ContrPromote在11个问题上击败了GEPA，在41个问题上追平了41个问题，在1个问题上输了。关于FiNER-139财务命名实体识别（Loukas等人，2022年），Contrapt比未优化的基线（+11.6%）达到+7.77 pp比GEPA增加1.94 pp（相对值增加2.66%），分支机构条件符合标准的美国公认会计原则金融工具类别。

2604.16625.title: AdaExplore: Failure-Driven Adaptation and Diversity-Preserving Search for Efficient Kernel Generation
2604.16625.abstract: arXiv:2604.16625v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Recent large language model (LLM) agents have shown promise in using execution feedback for test-time adaptation. However, robust self-improvement remains far from solved: most approaches still treat each problem instance independently, without accumulating reusable knowledge. This limitation is particularly pronounced in domain-specific languages such as Triton, which are underrepresented in LLM pretraining data. Their strict constraints and non-linear optimization landscape further make naive generation and local refinement unreliable. We propose AdaExplore, an agent framework that enables self-improvement via accumulated execution feedback for performance-critical kernel code generation through two complementary stages: failure-driven adaptation and diversity-preserving search, jointly improving correctness and optimization performance without additional fine-tuning or external knowledge. In the adaptation stage, the agent synthesizes tasks and converts recurring failures into a reusable memory of validity rules, helping subsequent generations remain within the feasible set. In the search stage, the agent organizes candidate kernels as a tree and alternates between small local refinements and larger structural regeneration, allowing it to explore the optimization landscape beyond local optima. Experiments on kernel runtime optimization benchmarks validate these gains: AdaExplore achieves 3.12x and 1.72x speedups on KernelBench Level-2 and Level-3, respectively, within 100 steps, and continues to improve with additional computation.
2604.16625.COMMENT: Proposes AdaExplore, a self-improving agent framework that accumulates execution feedback to adapt and search for efficient kernel generation, directly matching the self-evolving agent criterion.
2604.16625.title_cn: AdaExplore：故障驱动的适应和多样性保持搜索，以实现高效的内核生成
2604.16625.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.16625v1宣布类型：新摘要：最近的大型语言模型（LLM）代理在使用执行反馈进行测试时自适应方面表现出了希望。然而，稳健的自我改进仍然远未解决：大多数方法仍然独立处理每个问题实例，没有积累可重复使用的知识。这种限制在Triton等特定领域语言中尤其明显，这些语言在LLM预训练数据中代表性不足。它们严格的约束和非线性优化环境进一步使得朴素生成和局部细化不可靠。我们提出AdaExplore，这是一种代理框架，通过积累的执行反馈来实现自我改进，通过两个补充阶段生成对性能关键的内核代码：故障驱动的适应和多样性保留搜索，共同提高正确性和优化性能，而无需额外的微调或外部知识。在适应阶段，代理综合任务并将重复出现的失败转换为有效性规则的可重复使用记忆，帮助后代保持在可行集中。在搜索阶段，代理将候选核组织为树，并在小型局部细化和较大的结构再生之间交替，使其能够探索局部最优之外的优化景观。内核运行时优化基准测试的实验验证了这些收益：AdaExplore在100个步骤内分别在KernelBench Level-2和Level-3上实现了3.12x和1.72x的加速提升，并通过额外的计算继续改进。

authors: Jiaqi Wang (Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications
Haoge Deng (Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence)
Ting Pan (Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence)

2604.18518.title: UDM-GRPO: Stable and Efficient Group Relative Policy Optimization for Uniform Discrete Diffusion Models
2604.18518.abstract: arXiv:2604.18518v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Uniform Discrete Diffusion Model (UDM) has recently emerged as a promising paradigm for discrete generative modeling; however, its integration with reinforcement learning remains largely unexplored. We observe that naively applying GRPO to UDM leads to training instability and marginal performance gains. To address this, we propose \Ours, the first framework to integrate UDM with RL. Our method is guided by two key insights: (i) treating the final clean sample as the action provides more accurate and stable optimization signals; and (ii) reconstructing trajectories via the diffusion forward process better aligns probability paths with the pretraining distribution. Additionally, we introduce two strategies, Reduced-Step and CFG-Free, to further improve training efficiency. \Ours significantly improves base model performance across multiple T2I tasks. Notably, GenEval accuracy improves from $69\%$ to $96\%$ and PickScore increases from $20.46$ to $23.81$, achieving state-of-the-art performance in both continuous and discrete settings. On the OCR benchmark, accuracy rises from $8\%$ to $57\%$, further validating the generalization ability of our method. Code is available at \href{https://github.com/Yovecent/UDM-GRPO}{https://github.com/Yovecent/UDM-GRPO}.
2604.18518.COMMENT: Proposes a stable RL framework (UDM-GRPO) for training discrete diffusion models, directly aligning with RL methods for generative models.
2604.18518.title_cn: UDM-GRPO：均匀离散扩散模型的稳定有效的群体相对政策优化
2604.18518.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.18518v1宣布类型：新摘要：均匀离散扩散模型（UDM）最近成为离散生成式建模的一种有前途的范式;然而，其与强化学习的集成在很大程度上仍未被探索。我们观察到，天真地将GRPO应用于UDM会导致训练不稳定和边际性能收益。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了\我们的第一个将UDM与RL集成的框架。我们的方法由两个关键见解指导：（i）处理最终的干净样本，因为动作提供了更准确和稳定的优化信号;（ii）通过扩散正向过程重建轨迹更好地将概率路径与预训练分布对齐。此外，我们还引入了两种策略：Reduced-Step和CFG-Free，以进一步提高培训效率。\我们的产品显着提高了多个T2 I任务中的基本型号性能。值得注意的是，GenEval准确性从69美元提高到96美元，PickScore从20.46美元提高到23.81美元，在连续和离散设置中都实现了最先进的性能。在OCR基准上，准确性从8美元上升到57美元，进一步验证了我们方法的概括能力。代码可在\href{https：//github.com/Yovecent/UDM-GRPO}{https：//github.com/Yovecent/UDM-GRPO}上获取。

2604.17308.title: SkillFlow:Benchmarking Lifelong Skill Discovery and Evolution for Autonomous Agents
2604.17308.abstract: arXiv:2604.17308v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: As the capability frontier of autonomous agents continues to expand, they are increasingly able to complete specialized tasks through plug-and-play external skills. Yet current benchmarks mostly test whether models can use provided skills, leaving open whether they can discover skills from experience, repair them after failure, and maintain a coherent library over time. We introduce SkillFlow, a benchmark of 166 tasks across 20 families in which task construction within each family follows a Domain-Agnostic Execution Flow (DAEF) that defines an agent workflow framework, allowing these tasks to share a consistent workflow. Agents are evaluated under an Agentic Lifelong Learning protocol in which they begin without skills, solve tasks sequentially within each family, externalize lessons through trajectory- and rubric-driven skill patches, and carry the updated library forward. Experiments reveal a substantial capability gap. For Claude Opus 4.6, lifelong skill evolution improves task success from 62.65% to 71.08% (+8.43 points). However, high skill usage does not necessarily imply high utility: Kimi K2.5 gains only +0.60 points despite 66.87% skill usage, while Qwen-Coder-Next reaches only a 44.58% task completion rate and still regresses relative to the vanilla setting. SkillFlow contributes a structured testbed for this direction and an in-depth empirical analysis of skill discovery, patching, transfer, and their failure modes under lifelong evaluation.
2604.17308.COMMENT: Introduces a benchmark and protocol for lifelong skill discovery and evolution in agents, where agents externalize lessons as skill patches and carry an updated library forward, directly matching self-evolving agent criteria.
2604.17308.title_cn: SkillFlow：为自主代理的终身技能发现和进化进行基准
2604.17308.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17308v1宣布类型：新摘要：随着自主代理的能力前沿不断扩大，它们越来越能够通过即插即用的外部技能完成专业任务。然而，当前的基准测试主要测试模型是否可以使用所提供的技能，但对它们是否可以从经验中发现技能、在失败后修复技能以及随着时间的推移维护连贯的库留下了疑问。我们引入SkillFlow，这是一个涵盖20个系列的166项任务的基准，其中每个系列内的任务构建遵循定义代理工作流程框架的领域不可知执行流程（DAEF），允许这些任务共享一致的工作流程。代理人是在一个抽象的终身学习协议下进行评估的，其中他们在没有技能的情况下开始，在每个家庭内顺序解决任务，通过轨迹和标题驱动的技能补丁将课程外部化，并推进更新后的库。实验揭示了巨大的能力差距。对于Claude Opus 4.6来说，终身技能进步将任务成功率从62.65%提高到71.08%（+8.43分）。然而，高技能使用率并不一定意味着高效用：尽管技能使用率为66.87%，但Kimi K2.5仅获得+0.60分，而Qwen-Coder-Next仅达到44.58%的任务完成率，并且相对于香草设置仍在倒退。SkillFlow为此方向提供了结构化的测试平台，并对技能发现、修补、转移及其终身评估下的失败模式进行了深入的实证分析。

2604.17708.title: Co-evolving Agent Architectures and Interpretable Reasoning for Automated Optimization
2604.17708.abstract: arXiv:2604.17708v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Automating operations research (OR) with large language models (LLMs) remains limited by hand-crafted reasoning--execution workflows. Complex OR tasks require adaptive coordination among problem interpretation, mathematical formulation, solver selection, code generation, and iterative debugging. To address this limitation, we propose EvoOR-Agent, a co-evolutionary framework for automated optimization. The framework represents agent workflows as activity-on-edge (AOE)-style networks, making workflow topology, execution dependencies, and alternative reasoning paths explicit. On this representation, the framework maintains an architecture graph and evolves a population of reasoning individuals through graph-mediated path-conditioned recombination, multi-granularity semantic mutation, and elitist population update. A knowledge-base-assisted experience-acquisition module further injects reusable OR practices into initialization and semantic variation. Empirical results on heterogeneous OR benchmarks show that the proposed framework consistently improves over zero-shot LLMs, fixed-pipeline OR agents, and representative evolutionary agent frameworks. Case studies and ablation analyses further indicate that explicit architecture evolution and graph-supported reasoning-trajectory search contribute to both performance improvement and structural interpretability. These results suggest that treating agent architectures and reasoning trajectories as evolvable objects provides an effective route toward adaptive and interpretable automated optimization.
2604.17708.COMMENT: Introduces EvoOR-Agent, a co-evolutionary framework that evolves agent architectures and reasoning trajectories, enabling self-improving agents for automated optimization.
2604.17708.title_cn: 用于自动优化的协同进化代理体系结构和可解释推理
2604.17708.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17708v1宣布类型：新摘要：使用大型语言模型（LLM）自动化运营研究（OR）仍然受到手工推理--执行工作流程的限制。复杂的OR任务需要问题解释、数学公式、求解器选择、代码生成和迭代调试之间的自适应协调。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了EvoOR-Agent，一个自动优化的协同进化框架。该框架表示代理工作流的活动边缘（AOE）风格的网络，使工作流拓扑结构，执行依赖关系和替代推理路径明确。在此表示上，该框架保持一个架构图，并通过图介导的路径条件重组，多粒度语义突变和精英人口更新的推理个人的人口。一个知识库辅助的经验采集模块进一步注入可重用的或初始化和语义变化的做法。对异类OR基准的经验结果表明，所提出的框架始终优于零触发LLM、固定管道OR代理和代表性进化代理框架。案例研究和消融分析进一步表明，显式架构进化和图形支持的推理轨迹搜索有助于性能改进和结构可解释性。这些结果表明，将代理架构和推理轨迹视为可进化对象提供了一条实现自适应和可解释自动化优化的有效途径。

2604.18530.title: OGER: A Robust Offline-Guided Exploration Reward for Hybrid Reinforcement Learning
2604.18530.abstract: arXiv:2604.18530v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Recent advancements in Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) have significantly improved Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning, yet models often struggle to explore novel trajectories beyond their initial latent space. While offline teacher guidance and entropy-driven strategies have been proposed to address this, they often lack deep integration or are constrained by the model's inherent capacity. In this paper, we propose OGER, a novel framework that unifies offline teacher guidance and online reinforcement learning through a specialized reward modeling lens. OGER employs multi-teacher collaborative training and constructs an auxiliary exploration reward that leverages both offline trajectories and the model's own entropy to incentivize autonomous exploration. Extensive experiments across mathematical and general reasoning benchmarks demonstrate that OGER significantly outperforms competitive baselines, achieving substantial gains in mathematical reasoning while maintaining robust generalization to out-of-domain tasks. We provide a comprehensive analysis of training dynamics and conduct detailed ablation studies to validate the effectiveness of our entropy-aware reward modulation. Our code is available at https://github.com/ecoli-hit/OGER.git.
2604.18530.COMMENT: Proposes OGER, a novel RL framework with an offline-guided exploration reward for improving LLM reasoning, directly matching RL for LLMs criteria.
2604.18530.title_cn: OGER：一种用于混合强化学习的强大离线引导探索奖励
2604.18530.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.18530v1宣布类型：新摘要：带可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）的最新进展显着改进了大型语言模型（LLM）推理，但模型往往难以探索超出其初始潜在空间的新轨迹。虽然已经提出了线下教师指导和信息驱动策略来解决这个问题，但它们往往缺乏深度整合或受到模型固有能力的限制。在本文中，我们提出了OGER，这是一个新颖的框架，通过专门的奖励建模视角将线下教师指导和在线强化学习统一起来。OGER采用多教师协作培训并构建辅助探索奖励，利用离线轨迹和模型自身的信息量来激励自主探索。跨数学和一般推理基准的广泛实验表明，OGER的表现显着优于竞争基准，在数学推理方面取得了巨大进步，同时保持了对域外任务的稳健概括。我们对训练动态进行全面分析，并进行详细的消融研究，以验证我们的信息感知奖励调制的有效性。我们的代码可在https://github.com/ecoli-hit/OGER.git上获取。

2604.17739.title: Tool Learning Needs Nothing More Than a Free 8B Language Model
2604.17739.abstract: arXiv:2604.17739v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a prevalent paradigm for training tool calling agents, which typically requires online interactive environments. Existing approaches either rely on training data with ground truth annotations or require advanced commercial language models (LMs) to synthesize environments that keep fixed once created. In this work, we propose TRUSTEE, a data-free method training tool calling agents with dynamic environments fully simulated by free open-source LMs that can be as small as 8B, including task generation, user simulation, tool simulation and trajectory evaluation, paired with an adaptive curriculum learning mechanism that controls various aspects of the task difficulty dynamically during training. Our empirical results show that TRUSTEE brings consistent improvements across various domains and outperforms all the baselines which require extra external resources for training. These confirm that, with a sufficiently sophisticated design, even simulated environments with a local 8B LM as the backbone could set a strong baseline for tool learning, without expensive annotated data, realistic human interactions, executable tools or costly verifiable environments from human experts or commercial LMs. We hope our proposed paradigm could inspire future research on environment scaling with limited resources.
2604.17739.COMMENT: Proposes TRUSTEE, a data-free RL method for training tool-calling agents using dynamic environments simulated by small open-source LMs and an adaptive curriculum.
2604.17739.title_cn: 工具学习只需要免费的8B语言模型
2604.17739.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17739v1宣布类型：新摘要：强化学习（RL）已成为训练工具调用代理的流行范式，这通常需要在线交互环境。现有的方法要么依赖于具有基本真相注释的训练数据，要么需要高级商业语言模型（LM）来合成创建后保持固定的环境。在这项工作中，我们提出了TRUSEE，这是一种无数据方法训练工具，调用具有由免费开源LM（最小可达8B）完全模拟的动态环境的代理，包括任务生成、用户模拟、工具模拟和轨迹评估，并与自适应课程学习机制相结合，在训练期间动态控制任务难度的各个方面。我们的实证结果表明，TRUSEE在各个领域带来了一致的改进，并且优于所有需要额外外部培训资源的基线。这些证实了，通过足够复杂的设计，即使是以本地8B LM为骨干的模拟环境也可以为工具学习设置强大的基线，而无需昂贵的注释数据，逼真的人类交互，可执行工具或来自人类专家或商业LM的昂贵的可验证环境。我们希望我们提出的范例可以启发未来的研究环境缩放有限的资源。

2604.17290.title: Probabilistic Programs of Thought
2604.17290.abstract: arXiv:2604.17290v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: LLMs are widely used for code generation and mathematical reasoning tasks where they are required to generate structured output. They either need to reason about code, generate code for a given specification, or reason using programs of thought. The typical approach to code generation is to prompt the model and generate samples until an appropriate program is obtained. Within this process, sampling $n$ programs from the language model requires $n$ GPU compute-intensive generations which becomes prohibitively expensive for larger values of $n$. In this work, we address this limitation by exposing the LLM's distribution within the generated programs themselves. We propose a novel test-time framework we dub probabilistic programs of thought to obtain more samples from the model with fewer LLM generations. Given a program generated by a model and the associated next-token probabilities, we build a probabilistic program that compactly represents exponentially many deterministic programs. Since performing probabilistic reasoning in this probabilistic program is much cheaper, our approach allows sampling new programs without any additional GPU compute and little CPU overhead. We instantiate our approach on benchmarks for code generation, code understanding and mathematical reasoning and report improvements in performance with fewer generations from the LLM.
2604.17290.COMMENT: Proposes a test-time framework using probabilistic programs to compactly represent many deterministic reasoning traces, directly addressing latent reasoning efficiency.
2604.17290.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17290v1宣布类型：新摘要：LLM广泛用于需要生成结构化输出的代码生成和数学推理任务。他们要么需要对代码进行推理，为给定规范生成代码，要么使用思维程序进行推理。代码生成的典型方法是提示模型并生成样本，直到获得适当的程序。在此过程中，从语言模型中采样$n$程序需要$n$计算密集型的几代，对于较大的$n$值来说，这将变得非常昂贵。在这项工作中，我们通过在生成的程序本身中公开LLM的分布来解决这一限制。我们提出了一种新型的测试时框架，我们将其称为概率思维程序，以更少的LLM世代从模型中获得更多样本。给定由模型生成的程序和相关的下一个令牌概率，我们构建一个概率程序，该程序紧凑地以指数形式表示许多确定性程序。由于在这个概率程序中执行概率推理要便宜得多，因此我们的方法允许对新程序进行采样，而无需任何额外的图形处理器计算，也无需承担很少的中央处理器负担。我们实例化了代码生成、代码理解和数学推理的基准测试方法，并报告了LLM一代更少的性能改进。

2604.17501.title: CoAct: Co-Active LLM Preference Learning with Human-AI Synergy
2604.17501.abstract: arXiv:2604.17501v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Learning from preference-based feedback has become an effective approach for aligning LLMs across diverse tasks. However, high-quality human-annotated preference data remains expensive and scarce. Existing methods address this challenge through either self-rewarding, which scales by using purely AI-generated labels but risks unreliability, or active learning, which ensures quality through oracle annotation but cannot fully leverage unlabeled data. In this paper, we present CoAct, a novel framework that synergistically combines self-rewarding and active learning through strategic human-AI collaboration. CoAct leverages self-consistency to identify both reliable self-labeled data and samples that require oracle verification. Additionally, oracle feedback guides the model to generate new instructions within its solvable capability. Evaluated on three reasoning benchmarks across two model families, CoAct achieves average improvements of +13.25% on GSM8K, +8.19% on MATH, and +13.16% on WebInstruct, consistently outperforming all baselines.
2604.17501.COMMENT: Proposes CoAct, a novel preference learning framework combining self-rewarding and active learning to improve LLM alignment, directly matching RL for LLMs criteria.
2604.17501.title_cn: CoAct：协同LLM偏好学习与人机协同
2604.17501.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17501v1宣布类型：新摘要：从基于偏好的反馈中学习已成为在不同任务中协调LLM的有效方法。然而，高质量的人类注释偏好数据仍然昂贵且稀缺。现有的方法要么通过自我奖励（通过使用纯人工智能生成的标签进行扩展，但存在不可靠的风险），要么通过主动学习（通过Oracle注释确保质量，但无法完全利用未标记的数据）来解决这一挑战。在本文中，我们提出了CoAct，这是一个新颖的框架，通过战略性的人机协作将自我奖励和主动学习协同结合起来。CoAct利用自一致性来识别可靠的自标签数据和需要Oracle验证的样本。此外，Oracle反馈指导模型在其可解能力范围内生成新指令。根据两个模型系列的三个推理基准进行评估，CoAct在GSM 8K上的平均改进为+13.25%，在MATH上的平均改进为+8.19%，在WebDirect上的平均改进为+13.16%，始终优于所有基线。

2604.18578.title: Bounded Ratio Reinforcement Learning
2604.18578.abstract: arXiv:2604.18578v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) has become the predominant algorithm for on-policy reinforcement learning due to its scalability and empirical robustness across domains. However, there is a significant disconnect between the underlying foundations of trust region methods and the heuristic clipped objective used in PPO. In this paper, we bridge this gap by introducing the Bounded Ratio Reinforcement Learning (BRRL) framework. We formulate a novel regularized and constrained policy optimization problem and derive its analytical optimal solution. We prove that this solution ensures monotonic performance improvement. To handle parameterized policy classes, we develop a policy optimization algorithm called Bounded Policy Optimization (BPO) that minimizes an advantage-weighted divergence between the policy and the analytic optimal solution from BRRL. We further establish a lower bound on the expected performance of the resulting policy in terms of the BPO loss function. Notably, our framework also provides a new theoretical lens to interpret the success of the PPO loss, and connects trust region policy optimization and the Cross-Entropy Method (CEM). We additionally extend BPO to Group-relative BPO (GBPO) for LLM fine-tuning. Empirical evaluations of BPO across MuJoCo, Atari, and complex IsaacLab environments (e.g., Humanoid locomotion), and of GBPO for LLM fine-tuning tasks, demonstrate that BPO and GBPO generally match or outperform PPO and GRPO in stability and final performance.
2604.18578.COMMENT: Proposes a new RL framework (BRRL/BPO) with a provable monotonic improvement guarantee and extends it to LLM fine-tuning (GBPO), directly matching RL for LLMs criteria.
2604.18578.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.18578v1宣布类型：新摘要：近端策略优化（PPO）因其跨领域的可扩展性和经验鲁棒性而成为政策上强化学习的主要算法。然而，信任域方法的基本基础与PPO中使用的启发式剪辑目标之间存在显着脱节。在本文中，我们通过引入有界比强化学习（BRRL）框架来弥合这一差距。我们提出了一个新颖的正规化和约束政策优化问题，并推导出其分析最优解。我们证明这个解决方案可以确保单调性能改进。为了处理参数化的策略类，我们开发了一种名为有界策略优化（BPO）的策略优化算法，该算法可以最大限度地减少策略与BRRL的分析最优解决方案之间的加权差异。我们进一步根据BPO损失函数建立了所得政策的预期性能的下限。值得注意的是，我们的框架还提供了一个新的理论视角来解释PPO损失的成功，并将信任区政策优化和交叉熵方法（MBE）联系起来。我们还将BPO扩展到集团相关BPO（GBPO）以进行LLM微调。MuJoCo、Atari和复杂的IsaacLab环境中BPO的经验评估（例如，类人运动）以及用于LLM微调任务的GBPO证明BPO和GBPO在稳定性和最终性能方面通常匹配或优于PPO和GRPO。

2604.17982.title: Mitigating Multimodal Hallucination via Phase-wise Self-reward
2604.17982.abstract: arXiv:2604.17982v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) still struggle with vision hallucination, where generated responses are inconsistent with the visual input. Existing methods either rely on large-scale annotated data for fine-tuning, which incurs massive computational overhead, or employ static post-hoc strategies that overlook the dynamic nature of hallucination emergence. To address these, we introduce a new self-rewarding framework, enabling dynamic hallucination mitigation at inference time without external supervision. On the empirical side, we reveal that visual hallucination exhibits phase-wise dynamic patterns, peaking at the onset of each semantic phase. Drawing on these insights, we propose \textbf{PSRD} (\textbf{Phase-wise \textbf{S}elf-\textbf{R}eward \textbf{D}ecoding) for online hallucination correction guided by phase-wise self-reward signals. To reduce the cost of repeated self-evaluation during decoding, we distill the hallucination guidance signal from LVLMs into a lightweight reward model. The reward model subsequently provides on-the-fly guidance for targeted intervention during the decoding process, enabling precise hallucination suppression. The proposed PSRD significantly reduces the hallucination rate of LLaVA-1.5-7B by 50.0% and consistently outperforms existing post-hoc methods across five hallucination evaluation benchmarks for four LVLMs. Further analysis confirms that PSRD effectively mitigates hallucination propagation and achieves a highly controllable trade-off between strong performance and inference efficiency.
2604.17982.COMMENT: Proposes a self-rewarding framework (PSRD) for dynamic hallucination mitigation at inference time, aligning with RL for LLMs via a novel reward design and distillation loop.
2604.17982.title_cn: 通过分阶段自我奖励缓解多模式幻觉
2604.17982.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17982v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型视觉语言模型（LVLM）仍然与视觉幻觉作斗争，其中产生的反应与视觉输入不一致。现有的方法要么依赖大规模注释数据进行微调，这会产生大量的计算负担，要么采用静态事后策略，忽视了幻觉出现的动态性质。为了解决这些问题，我们引入了一种新的自我奖励框架，在没有外部监督的情况下在推理时间实现动态幻觉缓解。在经验方面，我们揭示了视幻觉表现出逐阶段的动态模式，在每个语义阶段的开始时达到峰值。根据这些见解，我们提出了\textBF{PSRD}（\textBF{Phase-wise \textBF{S}elf-\textBF{R}eward \textBF{D}ecoding），用于由阶段自我奖励信号引导的在线幻觉纠正。为了减少解码期间重复自我评估的成本，我们将LVLM中的幻觉引导信号提取到轻量级奖励模型中。奖励模型随后在解码过程中为有针对性的干预提供实时指导，从而实现精确的幻觉抑制。拟议的PSRD将LLaVA-1.5- 7 B的幻觉率显着降低了50.0%，并且在四种LVLM的五个幻觉评估基准中始终优于现有的事后方法。进一步分析证实PSRD有效地减轻了幻觉传播，并在强大的性能和推理效率之间实现了高度可控的权衡。

2604.16804.title: AutoOR: Scalably Post-training LLMs to Autoformalize Operations Research Problems
2604.16804.abstract: arXiv:2604.16804v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Optimization problems are central to decision-making in manufacturing, logistics, scheduling, and other industrial settings. Translating complicated descriptions of these problems into solver-ready formulations requires specialized operations research (OR) expertise, making it hard to scale. We present AutoOR, a scalable synthetic data generation and reinforcement learning pipeline that trains LLMs to autoformalize optimization problems specified in natural language across linear, mixed-integer, and non-linear categories. AutoOR generates verified training data from standard optimization forms and uses solver execution feedback as the reward signal for RL post-training. AutoOR applied to an 8B model achieves state-of-the-art or competitive results across six established OR benchmarks, matching significantly larger frontier models. For a non-linear problem class involving physical dynamics, where frontier models score near 0%, we introduce a curriculum RL strategy that bootstraps from limited initial training data to make this class tractable for post-training. We believe that methods such as AutoOR can significantly accelerate industrial decision-making with AI.
2604.16804.COMMENT: Proposes a scalable RL post-training pipeline for LLMs using solver execution feedback as a reward signal, directly aligning with RL for LLMs.
2604.16804.title_cn: AutoOR：可扩展的后培训LLM，以自动化运营研究问题
2604.16804.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.16804v1宣布类型：新摘要：优化问题是制造、物流、调度和其他工业环境中决策的核心。将这些问题的复杂描述转化为易于解决的公式需要专业的运筹学（OR）专业知识，因此难以扩展。我们提出了AutoOR，这是一个可扩展的合成数据生成和强化学习管道，可以训练LLM自动形式化以自然语言指定的线性，混合整数和非线性类别的优化问题。AutoOR从标准优化形式生成经过验证的训练数据，并使用求解器执行反馈作为RL后训练的奖励信号。应用于8B模型的AutoOR在六个已建立的OR基准中实现了最先进或具有竞争力的结果，与更大的前沿模型相匹配。对于涉及物理动力学的非线性问题类，其中前沿模型得分接近0%，我们引入了一种课程RL策略，该策略从有限的初始训练数据中引导，使该类易于后期训练。我们相信AutoOR等方法可以显着加速人工智能的工业决策。

2604.17405.title: STRIDE: Strategic Iterative Decision-Making for Retrieval-Augmented Multi-Hop Question Answering
2604.17405.abstract: arXiv:2604.17405v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Multi-hop question answering (MHQA) enables accurate answers to complex queries by retrieving and reasoning over evidence dispersed across multiple documents. Existing MHQA approaches mainly rely on iterative retrieval-augmented generation, which suffer from the following two major issues. 1) Existing methods prematurely commit to surface-level entities rather than underlying reasoning structures, making question decomposition highly vulnerable to lexical ambiguity. 2) Existing methods overlook the logical dependencies among reasoning steps, resulting in uncoordinated execution. To address these issues, we propose STRIDE, a framework that separates strategic planning, dynamic control, and grounded execution. At its core, a Meta-Planner first constructs an entity-agnostic reasoning skeleton to capture the abstract logic of the query, thereby deferring entity grounding until after the reasoning structure is established, which mitigates disambiguation errors caused by premature lexical commitment. A Supervisor then orchestrates sub-question execution in a dependency-aware manner, enabling efficient parallelization where possible and sequential coordination when necessary. By dynamically deciding whether to retrieve new evidence or infer from existing facts, it avoids redundant queries and error propagation, while fusing cross-branch information and reformulating failed queries to enhance robustness. Grounded fact extraction and logical inference are delegated to specialized execution modules, ensuring faithfulness through explicit separation of retrieval and reasoning. We further propose STRIDE-FT, a modular fine-tuning framework that uses self-generated execution trajectories from STRIDE, requiring neither human annotations nor stronger teacher models. Experiments show that STRIDE achieves robust and accurate reasoning, while STRIDE-FT effectively enhances open-source LLMs.
2604.17405.COMMENT: Proposes STRIDE-FT, a modular fine-tuning framework that uses self-generated execution trajectories for agent improvement, aligning with self-evolving agent criteria.
2604.17405.title_cn: FRAIDE：检索增强多跳问题回答的战略迭代决策
2604.17405.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.17405v1宣布类型：新摘要：多跳问答（MHQA）通过检索和推理分散在多个文档中的证据，可以准确地回答复杂的查询。现有的MHQA方法主要依赖于迭代检索增强生成，这存在以下两个主要问题。1)现有的方法过早地致力于表面实体而不是底层推理结构，使得问题分解极易受到词汇歧义的影响。2)现有的方法忽视了推理步骤之间的逻辑依赖性，导致执行不协调。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了WRIDE，这是一个将战略规划、动态控制和实地执行分开的框架。其核心是，元规划者首先构建一个实体不可知的推理框架来捕获查询的抽象逻辑，从而将实体基础推迟到推理结构建立之后，从而减轻了过早词汇承诺造成的歧义消除错误。然后，主管以依赖性感知的方式编排子问题执行，在可能的情况下实现高效并行化，并在必要时实现顺序协调。通过动态决定是检索新证据还是根据现有事实进行推断，它可以避免冗余查询和错误传播，同时融合跨分支信息并重新制定失败的查询以增强稳健性。扎根的事实提取和逻辑推理被委托给专门的执行模块，通过检索和推理的显式分离来确保忠实性。我们进一步提出CLARDE-FT，这是一个模块化的微调框架，它使用来自WRIDE的自生成执行轨迹，既不需要人类注释，也不需要更强的教师模型。实验表明，WRIDE实现了鲁棒、准确的推理，而CLARDE-FT则有效增强了开源LLM。

2604.16378.title: Reciprocal Co-Training (RCT): Coupling Gradient-Based and Non-Differentiable Models via Reinforcement Learning
2604.16378.abstract: arXiv:2604.16378v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) and classical machine learning methods offer complementary strengths for predictive modeling, yet their fundamentally different representations and training paradigms hinder effective integration: LLMs rely on gradient-based optimization over textual data, whereas models such as Random Forests (RF) employ non-differentiable feature partitioning. This work introduces a reciprocal co-training framework that couples an LLM with an RF classifier via reinforcement learning, creating an iterative feedback loop in which each model improves using signals from the other. Tabular data are reformulated into standardized textual representations for the LLM, whose embeddings augment the RF feature space, while calibrated RF probability estimates provide feedback signals that guide reinforcement learning updates of the LLM. Experiments across three medical datasets demonstrate consistent performance gains for both models, with particularly strong effects for the LLM. Ablation analyses show that iterative refinement, hybrid reward design, and dimensionality control jointly contribute to these gains. The proposed framework provides a general mechanism that allows incompatible model families to leverage each other's strengths through bidirectional adaptation.
2604.16378.COMMENT: Proposes a reciprocal co-training framework that couples an LLM with a non-differentiable model via reinforcement learning, creating an iterative feedback loop for mutual improvement.
2604.16378.title_cn: 互惠联合训练（RSC）：通过强化学习将基于对象的模型和不可区分的模型相结合
2604.16378.abstract_cn: arXiv：2604.16378v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型语言模型（LLM）和经典机器学习方法为预测建模提供了互补的优势，但它们从根本上不同的表示和训练范式阻碍了有效的集成：LLM依赖于对文本数据的基于梯度的优化，而随机森林（RF）等模型则采用不可微特征划分。这项工作引入了一个互惠的联合训练框架，该框架通过强化学习将LLM与RF分类器连接起来，创建一个迭代反馈循环，其中每个模型都使用来自另一个模型的信号进行改进。表格数据被重新表述为LLM的标准化文本表示，其嵌入增强了RF特征空间，而校准的RF概率估计提供了指导LLM的强化学习更新的反馈信号。在三个医学数据集上的实验表明，两种模型的性能都有一致的提高，其中LLM的效果尤其明显。消融分析表明，迭代细化，混合奖励设计，和维度控制共同有助于这些收益。建议的框架提供了一个通用的机制，允许不兼容的模型家庭，以利用彼此的优势，通过双向适应。
